tokens: Al, can you find the Earth?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Just look at the (LM's) S-band antenna and look up that way; it's right up there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll have to back up a little.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, where did we all agree was the best place to deploy this S-band?
Out the Y gear (that is, north), huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That ought to be...Here's a good spot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, I don't want to get too far away from the cable.
What's the matter with right here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Reading a decal on the S-Band package) "Point to Earth." That's what it says.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm glad you didn't land back about 50 feet (on the inner slope of Surveyor Crater).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, you can see some little shiny...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can also see some pure glass, if you look around.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can jump up in the air...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We got a lot of work to do.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I've got to do my 'fam' for 5 minutes here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: "Fam" doing some useful work, like getting the TV camera going.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) You really got to be careful when you go out of the light into the dark.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, I got a...This second thing won't come out.
Hey, give me a hand here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What can I do for you?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This second S-band thing (one of the two mast sections) won't deploy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, do you want me to pull or hold?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't think it's...Let go; let go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Doesn't that thing have two sections to it?
(Answering his own question) No; never mind; forget it; let's go.
Does the inner mast...It's just one piece, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You want to know if the inner mast is one or two sections.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It seems to me...I'm getting dumb-dumb, maybe.
(Pause) It looks shorter than it used to look at practice for some reason...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, that inner mast should be two sections.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Hearing Gibson) I think so; you have to pull out the top (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll lay this TV cable over here by the front porch.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Calling Al's attention to what he had done earlier in the EVA) That TV's almost off (the MESA mounting hardware).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You know, it's significantly easier just to do anything here (than on Earth).
Like this arm motion is so simple because you're not fighting the rest of your weight like we are there in one g.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughing heartily) Look at that; that (antenna) leg doesn't want to...Go on down, leg!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay; I'll get that TV down and show everybody.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Reading the antenna decal) "Lock inner mast; lock outer mast; extend and lock legs" - we've got.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, Houston; I'm going to move the TV camera now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, it's real nice moving around up here.
You don't seem to get tired.
You really hop like a bunny.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And it's pointing toward the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Dum dee dum, dum dum dum.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Having thrown something, probably either the carry bar or the rib protector) Look at that go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Ed; I was going to deploy this 20 feet at 10 (o'clock) but, because of the Sun being where it is, we're going to have to deploy it a little bit more toward the 2 o'clock position.
I think that will be okay, though.
That will give you a good shot; right in here.
I'll see if I can keep the Sun from getting in the camera at all.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we have a pretty bright image on the TV; ...
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: ...could you either move or stop it down?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, I'm going to have to stop it down.
(Pause) That's as far as it goes, Houston.
How does that look to you?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No, it still looks the same, Al.
Why don't you try shifting the scene?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, I'm going...The problem is the LM is very reflective.
Let me...Well, I got two choices.
Let me go over here further to the side, and you check and see if it reflects too much.
And if it does, I'll have to go stick it in the shade.
And then maybe shine past the LM.
Of course, that makes it not be too good either, but it may be the best we can do.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And also, you might try the automatic light control to the Out side.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, Al; watch (as I open the S-band umbrella).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I got to...How does that look, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We have a very bright image at the top and blacked out for about 80 percent of the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Laughing heartily as the S-band umbrella springs into position) Man, oh, man; did that thing deploy!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I'll tell you what let me do, Houston.
Let me move it around here so the back is to the Sun, and maybe that'll help.
Maybe that's the way we're going to have to do it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Once we learn the trick here, I think we can do it each time.
That may do it right there, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we haven't seen any change at all.
Why don't you go and put your glove in front of the lens, but not over it, to see whether we can get any change at all.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We've got a very bright part - about 20 percent of the top - and black on the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm pointing it at a non-bright area.
Let me point it away from the Sun here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, why don't you take a good close look at that lens and make sure it's in the right configuration.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I've got it on focus at infinity.
I've got the zoom at 30 or 40 or 50; I'll put it 75.
And I got the f-stop at 22.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I've got it pointing exactly opposite the Sun here, so...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we see no change at all in the scene.
Why don't you just give it a little tap?
It may be the color wheel is hung up.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I put this (S-band) antenna one foot too close.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Houston; it won't hurt if my PLSS antenna hits this S-band antenna (umbrella), will it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're going to have to help me line up this (S-band) antenna.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, I'm going to leave the camera just pointed off in the distance.
If you get any ideas, I'd be glad to work on it for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Try and point it off where you don't get any reflectance into it, and we'll be thinking about it here on the ground.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: The plug that runs right into the back of the TV, it's sort of a white plastic material and it looks like it's cracked and maybe even melted a little bit.
It doesn't look typical of that sort of connector.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Al, who has joined him at the S-band) Stand around on the back.
No, you got to go around.
Go around so you can look at the antenna and tell me when I've got it pointed at the Earth.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This thing...You're really going to have to watch that ALSEP.
You could tip over this whole antenna without even blinking at it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You've got to get around here.
The Earth and the Sun are right lined up.
You have to look right into the Sun and look up in the sky.
Watch it; you're on the TV antenna...I mean (TV) cable.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: See what I'm trying to do?
I've got to pitch over now, right?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I tell you where you need to go, Pete.
Now you need to point...Now you need to rotate the whole thing counter-clockwise (that is, aim it more to the north).
The old Earth's just hanging up there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, I'd say another 3 or 4 degrees (in azimuth), maybe.
That looks just good, as far as angles.
Now, up and down...I can move in a little closer, if that'd help you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think you're pretty close to right on there, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: All right, now wait a minute.
Don't let me knock it over, but I got to...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm trying to stabilize it for you (probably by holding one of the antenna legs)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, but every time you do, you push it in the (ground and, thereby, change the pointing).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) That's difficult, because it's so tender (that is, unstable) up here on these legs.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, I don't see the Earth anywhere in the sight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Have any ideas which way to go?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) I tell you, we're going to have to...Let's push these legs in a little bit (and) get this thing more stable.
Get this thing in the dirt.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Can you look right down the mast?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It looks like it's lined up to me.
You'll just have to move in and try it, because you're awfully close...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And I'll look for it in pitch.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Come a little bit more clockwise.
Now, run it up and down in pitch, and you're bound to get it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) go the other way, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I've got my hands off it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Be delicate, because this (possibly the crank) pulls on it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) You want me to go work on something else now?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just be careful you don't move in any closer, because the right side of your PLSS is going to bump it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll put out the solar wind collector (SWC).
(Pause) Before I do, let me get a (70mm) camera out here so I can take the picture (of the solar wind collector), Pete.
(Pause) Get out that solar wind collector.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Earth, I have you in the S-band antenna sight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: For me, it's solar wind collection (
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, when you finish up the solar wind, would you give one more last try on that (TV) camera?
Try opening the f-stop all the way and exercising the zoom.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Pete) Boy, we sure don't want to touch those (TV and S-band) cables.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No, we've got to stay away from those cables, you're right.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, just hang on to it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, how long we been out?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you've been out 1 hour and 2 minutes, and you're both running about 2 minutes off nominal - behind.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Looks like a good place for the solar wind collector, Pete.
I think I'll stick it right...(Straining) Boy.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's get back on this timeline in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Boy, I knew we were going to run late.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause as he orients the collector surface perpendicular to the Sun) That looks good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Want me to (garbled, either hang or bang) that TV first?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Those poles just drive right in.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now you can go whistle (that is, work) on the TV.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'll go work on it a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we copy your comment on the insertion of that (flag pole) into the ground.
How far in are you able to get it?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Oh, I pounded it in over a foot, I'd say; and it didn't look like it was any harder towards the end than right at the beginning.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, Houston, I'm going to move the focus (on the TV) a bit and see what happens.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Don't spend too much time on it.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I can feel that the wheel's...When I hold the end of the lens, I can feel the wheel's running, because I can feel something in motion inside.
Now, I just changed completely the settings I had before.
The f-stop's set at 22 but I can try something else.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, Al, we see no change down here.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, let me try another f-stop, the other way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There's no change down here, Al.
(Pause) That's coming in there, now, Al.
Okay, what change did you make?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I hit it on the top with my hammer.
I figured we didn't have a thing to lose.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I hit it on the top with this hammer I've got.
(Responding to Gibson) Yeah, that's skilled craftsmanship.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That part has already sheared this thing.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What should we do now with the lens, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; why don't you give us one more light rap (with the hammer), and also cut down on the f-stop?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Now, is the zoom right for you?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Need to jury rig (garbled under Gibson)...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, we're still not getting a good picture.
Why don't you press on, and we'll try to get back to it later, if we have time.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay; I'll pound it a little bit.
I'll just leave it like it is.
Point it slightly toward the LM here, so that, if you do get a picture, you'll see something.
That ought to give you some sort of a picture that you can think about.
I'd be glad to come back and work on it.
Got to go to work again.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What I need is a piece of tape.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, there's all kinds of tape around here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, the flag is (more or less) up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We hope everyone down there is as proud of it as we are...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We show you are very close to the nominal time line.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Like I said, hope everybody down there is as proud of it as we are to put it up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And we're proud of what you're doing.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Can we have a quickie (tourist photo) here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Al) Back up a little more ...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're getting into the TV cable!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Nope, I just went right over it, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't see you (because Al is standing up-Sun).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I got some pan shots (to do) and next the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Is Al doing all the work?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm heading out to do the pan photographs right now; and, with any luck at all, we'll get back on the timeline and complete what we need.
Al's taking shots of the solar wind.
And I'm hopping out here to the number 1 slot.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, you sure can move on this surface.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, but watch it when you hit a rock!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Counting frames and clock positions as he turns) 1, 2, 3.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Gibson) Who are you talking to?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, we have Clipper and you both on the same air-to-ground...
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I marked off Snowman with the telescope.
And we're going to get some good pictures from that one.
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Do you hear a little background noise, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, Houston, two of the pans are done.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, how was the LM inspection?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm working on it right now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (I'm) taking a look at that Surveyor, Al, I suspect we ought to be able to get there quite readily.
I'm going to head down into the crater a little bit for this set of pans...Whoops, (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Watch yourself; it's easy to slide.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, you can say that again.
(Pause) I notice you've been over here, haven't you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled) (Pause) I don't think this is going to show anything, but I'll give it a go.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, I'm trying to show the front gear there and how it planted itself, but it's not bright enough to (get a good photograph).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, do you have any comments on the footpad interaction with the surface?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Actually, these pads went in a little bit further than did Neil's.

I'd say most of the pads are in about an inch and a half to two; and it sort of looked like we were moving slightly forward (west), and had pretty well killed off our left/right velocity when we touched down.
The right-hand (north) footpad seems to have bounced.
That'd be the plus Y, the right-hand side one.
The others don't seem to have.
So it must have, maybe, hit there first, and rocked back and forth or something.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Do you see anything on the surface from the DPS (Descent Propulsion System, the descent engine)?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: The surface under there...It's kind of interesting, the surface under there is clean.
It doesn't have the loose dust particles as does the rest of the lunar surface about here.
It also has a number of small round dirt clods, if you want, that seem to be rolling off in a radial direction from underneath the skirt of the engine.
I'll take a couple of pictures (garbled) good shot, because the skirt's about 8 inches or so off the ground.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think I can get a good one for you, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, you can really move around out here, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean
